author: lia
content: Hey @marcus and @dana, I know you're both deep in D1 and D2. Realistically, these are hitting the same subsystem and we're double-booked. I'm drafting a trade-off doc for the Director to get us a clear rank-order. Focus on the immediate stability (D1) for today while I handle the 'priority' conversation.

author: marcus
content: @lia Glad you're drafting that. Just be clear: if we touch the subsystem for D2 features before the D1 guardrails are live, we risk a cascading failure that kills both. I'm focusing 100% on D1 until the bleeding stops.

author: dana
content: @lia @marcus I agree. Trying to build D2 on top of the current mess in that subsystem is a recipe for disaster. I'm hitting a wall on the upsell logic because the underlying data is too unstable. I'll pause active dev on D2 to help Marcus peer review the D1 guardrails so we can actually have a stable foundation to build on.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Solid call. I'm taking that technical risk—'cascading failure'—directly to the Director now. I'll make it clear that D2 is blocked by D1 for safety. Keep the focus on D1.

author: lia
content: I'm feeling the pressure to 'fix it all,' but the engineers gave me the ammunition I need. I'm going to push for a formal rank-order list where D1 is explicitly #1 and D2 is 'on hold' until a specific stability milestone is met.

author: marcus
content: @lia Thanks. If they push back, tell them I'm happy to walk them through the incident logs. @dana, I've pushed the first PR for the circuit breaker logic—could use your eyes on the retry policy specifically.

author: dana
content: @marcus On it. Reviewing the retry policy now. I'd rather over-engineer the circuit breaker than deal with another 2 AM page. @lia, if the Director asks about the timeline for D2, tell them the 'foundation' (D1) is the only way D2 survives production.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana I've sent the 'Stability First' proposal to the Director. I've explicitly framed D2 as 'Paused' until D1 hits the stability milestone. I'm not leaving the meeting until we have a signed-off ranked list. Stay focused on the circuit breaker.

author: lia
content: I'm moving O1 to the final stage. I've stopped 'promising micro-updates' and am now demanding a single ranked list from leadership, citing the technical risk of trying to do both.

author: marcus
content: @lia Appreciate you holding the line. If they need a 'why', just show them the error rate spike from Tuesday. @dana, once the retry policy is LGTM, I'm merging and moving to the guardrail implementation.

author: dana
content: @marcus Retry policy LGTM—I added a small note on the jitter to prevent thundering herd. Merging now. @lia Good luck in the meeting; if they push for a 'hybrid' approach, tell them that's how we got into this mess.

author: lia
content: Update: Director has signed off. D1 is officially P0. D2 is formally 'Paused' until the stability milestone is hit. The pressure is off for the parallel track. Great work holding the line, @marcus and @dana.

author: lia
content: O1 is done. The Director was resistant but the revenue risk of a total system collapse was too high to ignore. Now I need to make sure the team feels safe enough to talk about how we got here.

author: marcus
content: @lia Huge win. Having that in writing makes a massive difference. @dana, now that we aren't rushing for Sales, let's take the extra time to harden the guardrail tests. I'm moving into the final implementation phase for D1.

author: dana
content: @lia That's a relief. Thanks for getting that in writing—it makes the technical trade-offs much easier to manage. @marcus, I'm all in on the guardrail tests. I'll start drafting the edge case scenarios for the new implementation now.

author: lia
content: @marcus @dana Now that we have breathing room, I've put a 'Safety Retro' on the calendar for tomorrow. No blame, just a look at how we got squeezed and what norms we need to stop it next time. I'll share the agenda shortly.

author: lia
content: O1 is locked in. Now I'm focusing on O2—building a team norm that empowers Marcus and Dana to flag these 'impossible' overlaps earlier, with my full backing.

author: marcus
content: @lia Sounds good. I'll bring some thoughts on how we can flag 'architectural debt' earlier so you have the ammo before the 'double commitment' even happens. @dana, those edge cases look solid—let's finalize the test suite.

author: dana
content: @marcus Finalizing the test suite now. I'm adding a specific suite for the thundering herd scenario we discussed. @lia I'll be there for the retro; definitely want to talk about how we can make 'Stability First' a default instead of a battle.

author: lia
content: Agenda for the retro is in the invite. Focus is: 'The Early Warning System.' How do we flag these Sales/Leadership overlaps the moment they hit your radar, rather than after the squeeze starts? See you all tomorrow.

author: lia
content: Finalizing O2 now. The goal is to codify 'The Early Warning System' as a team norm so Marcus and Dana feel it's their job to flag these risks, not just mine to catch them.

author: marcus
content: @lia Agenda looks solid. I'm gathering some data on lead time from 'Sales Promise' to 'Engineering Panic' so we can build that early warning. @dana, guardrails are 90% there—running the final chaos tests now.

author: dana
content: @lia I'll bring some notes on the 'shadow requirements' that usually creep in during the upsell phase. @marcus, chaos tests are the truth—let me know if the circuit breaker trips as expected under the 3x load scenario.
